text: Kubernetes keeps containerized services running by scheduling pods, restarting failed workloads, and coordinating rolling updates across cluster nodes.
Kubeflow Pipelines packages each workflow step as a container task, passes artifacts between tasks, and records run state in the pipeline UI.
MLflow stores parameters, metrics, and artifacts for each run so training and evaluation results can be compared over time.
MinIO provides S3-compatible object storage for checkpoints, scorecards, and other MLflow artifacts in this repository.
Continued pretraining adapts a base language model with next-token prediction on unlabeled domain text rather than on instruction labels.
A continued pretraining dataset should be split into train, validation, and test partitions so perplexity and loss are measured on held-out text.
Validation loss helps detect whether a model is improving on domain text, while test loss is reserved for a final unbiased quality check.
When serving Qwen locally on a MacBook, smaller checkpoints and conservative generation settings are more practical than large CPU-bound deployments.
A baseline endpoint represents the stable model in production, while a candidate endpoint serves the new model that is being evaluated for promotion.
A scorecard summarizes benchmark metrics such as public score, domain score, and overall score, while a model card explains what was evaluated and how.
Golden-set prompts should cover the domain concepts the model must answer correctly, and each prompt should include reference facts or keywords for scoring.
For release gating, evaluate the served candidate endpoint and the baseline endpoint separately so quality and serving behavior are both validated.
